module1.clean_text:
RE-HOSPITALIZATION REPORT
This is the child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] years old from Mascara and living in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, monitored at our level since [DATE] for hyper IgE type syndromic immunodeficiency admitted today for his 08th venoglobulin treatment
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: hyper IgE type immunodeficiency. Associated diagnosis: epilepsy with neurodevelopmental disorder under treatment. Age of diagnosis: [AGE] years and 7 [AGE] of diagnosis: [DATE] The diagnosis of primary immunodeficiency type hyper IgE is suspected based on the following criteria:
Anamnestics:
A young death in the family. Family history of primary immunodeficiency. CLINIC:
Chronic fungal skin infections from the age of [AGE] such as onychomycosis. A personal and family atopy. Paraclinic:
Assessment of the immunodeficiency carried out on [DATE] (attached) initial finding in favor of a suspicion of an immunodeficiency type reduction of Th17 with gain of function of the STAT 1 gene. (To be completed by a phosphorylation test of the STAT 1 gene)
IgG: 6.99 g/l IgA: 0.34 g/l IgM: 0.57 g/l
IgE: 390 IU/ml (high)
TH 17: 0.23% (decreased). The STAT 1 phosphorylation test: done on: [DATE] (attached) returning: correct phosphorylation of the STAT 1 gene. The STAT 3 phosphorylation test done on: [DATE] (attached): returning in favor of correct STAT 3 phosphorylation (does not eliminate the diagnosis of hyper IgE syndrome). Hospitalization history:
Processing in progress:
Bactrim: 10 mg/kg/day. Itraconazole: 10 mg/kg/day. Events since last visit: [DATE] No
On the respiratory level: without abnormalities. On the digestive level: preserved transit, preserved appetite. On the skin: whitlow lesion in good clinical progress following cessation of antibiotic prophylaxis for 3 days
Neurologically: (language and behavior): improvement in the child's language (he says words and sentences coherently, with a reduction in hyperactivity. Reason for hospitalization: admitted today for 8th course of venoglobulins. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 16 kg (-0.41 DS)
Size: 98 cm (-0.86 DS)
PC: 49 cm (-1.33 DS)
BMI: 16.1 (+0.61 SD)
Gomez: 97% (normal nutritional status). General condition preserved, afebrile: T° 36.1 C, integuments and conjunctiva normal-colored. Head and neck examination: broad nasal base
ENT examination: throat: clean, no rhinorrhea or otorrhea. Examination of the oral cavity: absence of thrush or dental abscess. Pleuropulmonary examination:
Absence of thoracic deformation. Eupneic FR: 25 cycles/min. clear auscultation in both pulmonary fields. SpO2: 99% under ambient air. Cardiovascular examination: B1 B2 clearly audible, no breathing or added noises. HR: 100 beats/min. Abdominopelvic examination: absence of scar or collateral circulation. Soft abdomen, absence of organomegaly, preserved transit. Genital examination: male morphotype, no anomalies of sexual differentiation. Neurological examination: conscious, cooperating child, absence of convulsions, improvement in language (he says words with sentences in a coherent manner), with regression of motor hyperactivity, cessation of antiepileptic treatment. Osteoarticular examination: ligamentous hyperlaxity, no skeletal abnormalities
Examination of the skin and appendages: No onychomycotic fungal lesions, whitlow lesion healing. The rest of the clinical examination was without abnormalities. BIOLOGY:
NFS:
GB: 8540 elm/mm3 Hb: 13 g/dl Plq: 276000 elm/mm3
CRP: negative
Residual IgG level 02/15/2026: 10.76 IU/ml
Coproparasitology + dermatology opinion requested: not done
CAT:
Hospitalization in day hospital
Conditioning
Request for assessment: CBC, CRP, residual immunoglobulin dosage
Infusion of multivalent immunoglobulins No. 8: 0.6g/kg or 10g
Hygiene-dietary measures (therapeutic education, notebook keeping)
Close monitoring
The child did not present any side effects (no headaches, no hypo or high blood pressure, no allergic reaction).
Dismissed today after end of treatment with prophylactic treatment (Bactrim and Itraconazole) with appointment for the 9th treatment
The exams requested:
Request an updated report on follow-up in Neuropediatrics.

phenopacket.input:
RE-HOSPITALIZATION REPORT
This is the child Bouteldja Mohamed El Amir aged 04 years old from Mascara and living in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, monitored at our level since 06/30/2025 for hyper IgE type syndromic immunodeficiency admitted today for his 08th venoglobulin treatment
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: hyper IgE type immunodeficiency. Associated diagnosis: epilepsy with neurodevelopmental disorder under treatment. Age of diagnosis: 03 years and 7 months
Date of diagnosis: 02/06/2025. The diagnosis of primary immunodeficiency type hyper IgE is suspected based on the following criteria:
Anamnestics:
A young death in the family. Family history of primary immunodeficiency. CLINIC:
Chronic fungal skin infections from the age of 9 months such as onychomycosis. A personal and family atopy. Paraclinic:
Assessment of the immunodeficiency carried out on 02/06/2025 (attached) initial finding in favor of a suspicion of an immunodeficiency type reduction of Th17 with gain of function of the STAT 1 gene. (To be completed by a phosphorylation test of the STAT 1 gene)
IgG: 6.99 g/l IgA: 0.34 g/l IgM: 0.57 g/l
IgE: 390 IU/ml (high)
TH 17: 0.23% (decreased). The STAT 1 phosphorylation test: done on: 07/21/2025 (attached) returning: correct phosphorylation of the STAT 1 gene. The STAT 3 phosphorylation test done on: 07/27/2025 (attached): returning in favor of correct STAT 3 phosphorylation (does not eliminate the diagnosis of hyper IgE syndrome). Hospitalization history:
Processing in progress:
Bactrim: 10 mg/kg/day. Itraconazole: 10 mg/kg/day. Events since last visit: 01/15/2026
Consultations: No
On the respiratory level: without abnormalities. On the digestive level: preserved transit, preserved appetite. On the skin: whitlow lesion in good clinical progress following cessation of antibiotic prophylaxis for 3 days
Neurologically: (language and behavior): improvement in the child's language (he says words and sentences coherently, with a reduction in hyperactivity. Reason for hospitalization: admitted today for 8th course of venoglobulins. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 16 kg (-0.41 DS)
Size: 98 cm (-0.86 DS)
PC: 49 cm (-1.33 DS)
BMI: 16.1 (+0.61 SD)
Gomez: 97% (normal nutritional status). General condition preserved, afebrile: T° 36.1 C, integuments and conjunctiva normal-colored. Head and neck examination: broad nasal base
ENT examination: throat: clean, no rhinorrhea or otorrhea. Examination of the oral cavity: absence of thrush or dental abscess. Pleuropulmonary examination:
Absence of thoracic deformation. Eupneic FR: 25 cycles/min. clear auscultation in both pulmonary fields. SpO2: 99% under ambient air. Cardiovascular examination: B1 B2 clearly audible, no breathing or added noises. HR: 100 beats/min. Abdominopelvic examination: absence of scar or collateral circulation. Soft abdomen, absence of organomegaly, preserved transit. Genital examination: male morphotype, no anomalies of sexual differentiation. Neurological examination: conscious, cooperating child, absence of convulsions, improvement in language (he says words with sentences in a coherent manner), with regression of motor hyperactivity, cessation of antiepileptic treatment. Osteoarticular examination: ligamentous hyperlaxity, no skeletal abnormalities
Examination of the skin and appendages: No onychomycotic fungal lesions, whitlow lesion healing. The rest of the clinical examination was without abnormalities. BIOLOGY:
NFS:
GB: 8540 elm/mm3 Hb: 13 g/dl Plq: 276000 elm/mm3
CRP: negative
Residual IgG level 02/15/2026: 10.76 IU/ml
Coproparasitology + dermatology opinion requested: not done
CAT:
Hospitalization in day hospital
Conditioning
Request for assessment: CBC, CRP, residual immunoglobulin dosage
Infusion of multivalent immunoglobulins No. 8: 0.6g/kg or 10g
Hygiene-dietary measures (therapeutic education, notebook keeping)
Close monitoring
The child did not present any side effects (no headaches, no hypo or high blood pressure, no allergic reaction).
Dismissed today after end of treatment with prophylactic treatment (Bactrim and Itraconazole) with appointment for the 9th treatment
The exams requested:
Request an updated report on follow-up in Neuropediatrics.
